@@ -0,0 +1,76 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "flagger-499-traefik-support",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "flagger: Traefik support",
+    "description": "Traefik support. Requested by 35+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current flagger deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your flagger version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger\nhelm list -n flagger 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working flagger installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review flagger configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant flagger configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger\nkubectl get configmap -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flagger\n```\nI see in the docs references to numerous other ingress controller but Traefik is not mentioned, it can do canaries via annotations. Is it not supported by Flagger?"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Traefik support",
+        "description": "fix: #499 \n\nTraefik has a [TraefikService](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/routing/services/) crd resource, which enables us to do weighted load balancing between multiple kubernetes services.\n\nThe only nuance that I've stumbled upon is that `traefik` stops to recognize `TraefikService` if one of the services in it have 0 endpoints, which is the case for promoted canary. So I have to remove canary service from `TraefikService` when canary weight is set to 0.\n\n#### What has been done:\n- Added\n\nSee the fix PR for details: linked PR"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger\nkubectl get events -n flagger --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Traefik support\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "fix: #499 \n\nTraefik has a [TraefikService](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/routing/services/) crd resource, which enables us to do weighted load balancing between multiple kubernetes services.\n\nThe only nuance that I've stumbled upon is that `traefik` stops to recognize `TraefikService` if one of the services in it have 0 endpoints, which is the case for promoted canary.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "flagger",
+      "graduated",
+      "app-definition",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "flagger"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Ingress",
+      "Service"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "beginner",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "graduated",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/fluxcd/flagger/issues/499",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/fluxcd/flagger",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/fluxcd/flagger/pull/736"
+    },
+    "reactions": 35,
+    "comments": 1,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with flagger installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
